docs.title: netskope-sdwan-py-sdk
docs.text:
netskope-sdwan-py-sdk is an unofficial, read-only Python SDK for Netskope SD-WAN APIs.

It is designed for practical API access in scripts, diagnostics, and internal tooling:

  • GET endpoints only
  • v2-first client surface
  • explicit client.v1.* namespace for legacy v1-only endpoints
  • lightweight models with minimal transformation

The SDK stays intentionally close to the backend API so filters, IDs, and response payloads remain understandable when you move between code, examples, and API documentation.

docs.title: What It Covers
docs.text:
• Gateway operations through client.gateways
  • Read-only v2 resources such as users, user groups, policies, segments, cloud accounts, software, and certificates
  • Special GET helpers such as software downloads, site command output, and gateway telemetry overview
  • Transitional v1 managers under client.v1.edges, client.v1.monitoring, and client.v1.users

docs.title: What It Does Not Cover
docs.text:
• POST, PUT, PATCH, or DELETE
  • async client support
  • code generation
  • automatic DNS or CNAME discovery for tenant resolution

Start with Getting Started for installation and a first request.

docs.title: API Token
docs.text:
The SDK uses bearer token authentication. Provide the token directly:

from netskopesdwan import SDWANClient

client = SDWANClient(
    base_url="https://your-sdwan-tenant.api.eu.infiot.net",
    api_token="TOKEN",
)

Or through the environment:

export NETSKOPESDWAN_API_TOKEN="TOKEN"

docs.title: Base URL
docs.text:
The simplest option is to pass a direct SD-WAN API host through base_url:

client = SDWANClient(
    base_url="https://legacy123.api.eu.infiot.net",
    api_token="TOKEN",
)

You can also set:

export NETSKOPESDWAN_BASE_URL="https://legacy123.api.eu.infiot.net"

docs.title: Tenant URL Resolution
docs.text:
Supported goskope.com tenant URLs can be resolved when the SD-WAN tenant name is known:

client = SDWANClient(
    tenant_url="customer.de.goskope.com",
    sdwan_tenant_name="legacy123",
    api_token="TOKEN",
)

Resolution remains intentionally conservative:

  • direct infiot.net API URLs are preferred
  • supported goskope suffix mapping is available
  • automatic DNS and CNAME discovery are not implemented

docs.title: Optional Timeout
docs.text:
You can set a timeout directly:

client = SDWANClient(
    base_url="https://legacy123.api.eu.infiot.net",
    api_token="TOKEN",
    timeout=30,
)

Or with:

export NETSKOPESDWAN_TIMEOUT="30"

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: The repository includes a few small runnable examples that reflect the current SDK surface.

docs.title: List Gateways
docs.text:
python examples/basic_gateways.py

Shows:

  • client initialization
  • listing all gateways
  • filtering online gateways with filter="status:up"
  • friendly handling for invalid placeholder tenant URLs

docs.title: Gateway Telemetry Overview
docs.text:
python examples/basic_gateway_telemetry.py

Shows:

  • selecting a gateway
  • calling client.gateways.get_telemetry_overview(...)
  • printing the raw telemetry payload

docs.title: Legacy v1 Monitoring
docs.text:
python examples/basic_monitoring.py

Shows:

  • selecting a gateway, preferring active gateways
  • using client.v1.monitoring
  • running monitoring calls with a short UTC time window
  • printing raw monitoring payloads

docs.title: Full Smoke Runner
docs.text:
python examples/smoke_all_gets.py

This script is meant for manual validation across the supported GET surface. It prints concise PASS, SKIP, and FAIL results and exits non-zero when an attempted endpoint fails.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text: • Python 3.11 or newer

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Install the package:

pip install netskope-sdwan-py-sdk

For local development:

python3.11 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -e ".[dev]"

To build the documentation locally:

pip install -e ".[docs]"
mkdocs serve

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
from netskopesdwan import SDWANClient

client = SDWANClient(
    base_url="https://your-sdwan-tenant.api.eu.infiot.net",
    api_token="TOKEN",
)

gateways = client.gateways.list()
for gateway in gateways:
    print(gateway.id, gateway.name)

docs.title: Environment-Based Configuration
docs.text:
You can also configure the client through environment variables:

export NETSKOPESDWAN_BASE_URL="https://your-sdwan-tenant.api.eu.infiot.net"
export NETSKOPESDWAN_API_TOKEN="TOKEN"

from netskopesdwan import SDWANClient

client = SDWANClient()
print(client.gateways.list())

docs.title: Common Notes
docs.text:
• Placeholder hosts such as https://customer.api.eu.infiot.net are examples only
  • client.audit_events.list(...) requires bounded created_at_from and created_at_to filters
  • client.site_commands.get_output(...) returns a DownloadResult

docs.title: Reference Values
docs.text: This page collects enum-backed values from the uploaded OpenAPI files that show up in the current SDK GET surface.

docs.title: v2 other GET response enums
docs.text:
• GatewayGroup.type: hub, spoke
  • Policy.type: client, gateway
  • User.controlled: manual, sso, x509, idp
  • VpnPeer.type: IKEV2, GRE

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
This section documents the package-supported Python API using mkdocstrings.

The reference is organized by module so it stays maintainable as the SDK grows:

  • package exports and top-level entry points
  • client configuration and tenant resolution helpers
  • resource manager modules
  • legacy v1 namespace
  • models, enums, and exceptions

Internal helper modules are intentionally kept light in the navigation. The focus here is the supported SDK surface that callers are most likely to import or interact with directly.

docs.title: SDWANClient
docs.text:
Entry point for the Netskope SD-WAN SDK.

The client loads configuration from constructor arguments or environment variables, resolves the effective API base URL, and exposes supported resource managers as attributes such as gateways, users, and v1.monitoring.

docs.title: __init__(*, tenant_url=None, base_url=None, sdwan_tenant_name=None, api_token=None, timeout=None, verify_ssl=True)
docs.text:
Initialize a read-only SDK client.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default tenant_url str | None

Optional goskope tenant URL used for supported tenant resolution flows.

None base_url str | None

Direct SD-WAN API base URL such as https://tenant.api.eu.infiot.net.

None sdwan_tenant_name str | None

Optional SD-WAN tenant name used with supported goskope inputs.

None api_token str | None

API bearer token.

None timeout float | int | None

Optional requests timeout in seconds.

None verify_ssl bool

Whether to verify TLS certificates for outgoing requests.

True

docs.title: ClientSettings dataclass
docs.text: Normalized client configuration after constructor and environment resolution.

docs.title: load_settings(*, base_url, tenant_url, api_token, timeout, verify_ssl, sdwan_tenant_name)
docs.text: Resolve client settings from explicit arguments and supported environment variables.

docs.title: Gateway Manager Module
docs.text:
The sections below summarize the GET methods backed by the uploaded v2 OpenAPI file.

Path parameter names follow the OpenAPI schema. In Python, these IDs are usually passed positionally, for example client.gateways.get("gw-123").

GatewayManager.get_telemetry_overview(...) is intentionally not expanded here because it is currently exposed by the SDK but not described in the uploaded official OpenAPI files.

docs.title: gateways.list
docs.text: Returns gateways.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• after: optional cursor for the next page
  • first: optional maximum number of results
  • sort: optional backend sort expression
  • filter: optional backend filter expression such as status:up

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text:
• top-level response includes data and page_info
  • the SDK stores the last page_info value on client.gateways.last_page_info
  • gateway objects include fields such as id, name, is_activated, managed, overlay_id, created_at, modified_at, and device_config_raw

docs.title: gateways.get
docs.text: Returns one gateway by ID.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: • id: required gateway ID

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text: • gateway objects include fields such as id, name, is_activated, managed, overlay_id, created_at, modified_at, and device_config_raw

docs.title: gateways.get_localui_password
docs.text: Returns the local UI password payload for a gateway.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: • id: required gateway ID

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text:
• response is a top-level object
  • key fields include calculated and config

docs.title: gateways.get_ssh_password
docs.text: Returns the SSH password payload for a gateway.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: • id: required gateway ID

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text:
• response is a top-level object
  • key fields include calculated and config

Read-only manager for /v2/gateways endpoints.

docs.title: get(gateway_id)
docs.text: Fetch one gateway by ID.

docs.title: get_localui_password(gateway_id)
docs.text: Fetch the local UI password payload for a gateway.

docs.title: get_ssh_password(gateway_id)
docs.text: Fetch the SSH password payload for a gateway.

docs.title: get_telemetry_overview(gateway_id)
docs.text: Fetch the raw telemetry overview payload for a gateway.

docs.title: list(*, after=None, first=None, sort=None, filter=None)
docs.text:
List gateways.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default after str | None

Cursor used for forward pagination.

None first int | None

Maximum number of items to return.

None sort str | None

Raw backend sort expression.

None filter str | None

Raw backend filter expression such as status:up.

None

docs.title: Resource Manager Module
docs.text:
The sections below cover the GET-backed resource manager methods that are described by the uploaded v2 OpenAPI file.

For most v2 list methods, the SDK mirrors the same query parameters as the API: after, first, sort, and filter. Path IDs are usually passed positionally in Python.

audit_events.list(...) is the one notable SDK-level wrapper: the OpenAPI only exposes filter, but the SDK requires created_at_from and created_at_to and composes the bounded filter for you.

docs.title: address_groups.list
docs.text: Returns address groups.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• after: optional cursor for the next page
  • first: optional maximum number of results
  • sort: optional backend sort expression
  • filter: optional backend filter expression

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text:
• top-level response includes data and page_info
  • key item fields include id, name, description, usage_type, metadata_tags, created_at, and modified_at
  • usage_type uses the AddressGroupUsageType enum values listed in reference-values.md

docs.title: address_groups.get
docs.text: Returns one address group by ID.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: • id: required address group ID

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text: • key fields include id, name, description, usage_type, metadata_tags, created_at, and modified_at

docs.title: address_groups.list_address_objects
docs.text: Returns address objects that belong to an address group.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• group-id: required address group ID
  • after: optional cursor for the next page
  • first: optional maximum number of results
  • sort: optional backend sort expression
  • filter: optional backend filter expression

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text:
• top-level response includes data and page_info
  • key item fields include id, name, address, type, group_ids, site_id, segment_id, and tags
  • type uses the AddressObjectType enum values listed in reference-values.md

docs.title: audit_events.list
docs.text: Returns audit event records within a bounded time range.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• created_at_from: required SDK argument that contributes the lower bound of the event-time filter
  • created_at_to: required SDK argument that contributes the upper bound of the event-time filter
  • type: optional SDK argument appended to the filter; values come from the audit type enum in reference-values.md
  • subtype: optional SDK argument appended to the filter; values come from the audit subtype enum in reference-values.md
  • activity: optional SDK argument appended to the filter; the spec also defines this as a large enum of event constants
  • after: optional cursor for the next page
  • first: optional maximum number of results
  • sort: optional backend sort expression
  • filter: optional raw filter expression merged with the bounded time range

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text:
• top-level response includes data and page_info
  • key item fields include id, event_time, type, subtype, activity, actor_nrn, target_nrn, note, and diff

  • sort: optional backend sort expression
  • filter: optional backend filter expression

docs.title: Response notes

• top-level response includes data and page_info
  • key item fields include id, name, description, servers, is_default, created_at, and modified_at

docs.title: SDWANClient
docs.text:
Entry point for the Netskope SD-WAN SDK.

The client loads configuration from constructor arguments or environment variables, resolves the effective API base URL, and exposes supported resource managers as attributes such as gateways, users, and v1.monitoring.

docs.title: __init__(*, tenant_url=None, base_url=None, sdwan_tenant_name=None, api_token=None, timeout=None, verify_ssl=True)
docs.text:
Initialize a read-only SDK client.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default tenant_url str | None

Optional goskope tenant URL used for supported tenant resolution flows.

None base_url str | None

Direct SD-WAN API base URL such as https://tenant.api.eu.infiot.net.

None sdwan_tenant_name str | None

Optional SD-WAN tenant name used with supported goskope inputs.

None api_token str | None

API bearer token.

None timeout float | int | None

Optional requests timeout in seconds.

None verify_ssl bool

Whether to verify TLS certificates for outgoing requests.

True

docs.title: V1MonitoringWANMetric
docs.text:
Bases: StrEnum

Allowed WAN metric values for v1 monitoring path link queries.

docs.title: Page dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Generic[T]

Minimal pagination container for future expansion.

docs.title: ResolutionResult dataclass
docs.text: Result of resolving a tenant input into a concrete SD-WAN API base URL.

docs.title: to_metadata()
docs.text: Return the resolution result as a plain metadata dictionary.

docs.title: normalize_url(value)
docs.text: Normalize a tenant or API host into an HTTPS URL string.

docs.title: resolve_api_base_url(*, base_url, tenant_url, sdwan_tenant_name)
docs.text: Resolve a direct base URL or supported tenant URL into an API endpoint.

docs.title: Transport
docs.text: Thin synchronous HTTP transport used by the SDK managers.

docs.title: get(path, *, params=None)
docs.text: Perform a GET request and decode a JSON response.

docs.title: get_download(path, *, params=None)
docs.text: Perform a GET request and return a binary download payload.

docs.title: get_text(path, *, params=None)
docs.text: Perform a GET request and return the raw text body.

docs.title: Legacy v1 Module
docs.text:
The sections below summarize the GET methods backed by the uploaded v1 OpenAPI file.

The v1 spec uses camelCase wire parameter names such as afterCursor and childTenantId. In Python, the SDK exposes snake_case equivalents such as after_cursor and child_tenant_id.

docs.title: v1.edges.list
docs.text: Returns v1 edges.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• after_cursor (afterCursor): optional cursor for records after the current page
  • before_cursor (beforeCursor): optional cursor for records before the current page
  • max_items (maxItems): optional maximum number of results
  • child_tenant_id (childTenantId): optional child tenant selector
  • get_templates (getTemplates): optional flag to include template records

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text:
• top-level response includes data, startCursor, endCursor, firstPage, and lastPage
  • key edge fields include id, description, model, role, interfaces, activated, assignedPolicy, dateCreated, and dateModified
  • edge model and role values are listed in reference-values.md

docs.title: v1.edges.get
docs.text: Returns one v1 edge by ID.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• edge_id (edgeId): required edge ID
  • child_tenant_id (childTenantId): optional child tenant selector

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text: • key fields include id, description, model, role, interfaces, activated, assignedPolicy, dateCreated, and dateModified

docs.title: v1.edges.list_interfaces
docs.text: Returns interfaces for an edge.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• edge_id (edgeId): required edge ID
  • child_tenant_id (childTenantId): optional child tenant selector

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text:
• response is an array of interface objects
  • key fields include name, mode, type, addresses, macAddr, enableNat, isDisabled, and lteProps

docs.title: v1.edges.get_interface
docs.text: Returns one edge interface by name.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• edge_id (edgeId): required edge ID
  • interface_name (interfaceName): required interface name
  • child_tenant_id (childTenantId): optional child tenant selector

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text: • key fields include name, mode, type, addresses, macAddr, enableNat, isDisabled, and lteProps

docs.title: v1.edges.list_gateway_interfaces
docs.text: Returns gateway interfaces for a v1 gateway/edge ID.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• edge_id (edgeId): required gateway ID in the v1 path shape
  • child_tenant_id (childTenantId): optional child tenant selector

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text:
• response is an array of interface objects
  • key fields include name, mode, type, addresses, macAddr, enableNat, isDisabled, and lteProps

docs.title: v1.edges.get_gateway_interface
docs.text: Returns one gateway interface by name.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• edge_id (edgeId): required gateway ID in the v1 path shape
  • interface_name (interfaceName): required interface name
  • child_tenant_id (childTenantId): optional child tenant selector

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text: • key fields include name, mode, type, addresses, macAddr, enableNat, isDisabled, and lteProps

docs.title: v1.monitoring.get_device_flows_totals
docs.text: Returns device flow totals for a gateway and time range.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• gateway_id (gatewayId): required gateway ID
  • child_tenant_id (childTenantId): optional child tenant selector
  • start_datetime (startDatetime): required ISO 8601 UTC start timestamp
  • end_datetime (endDatetime): required ISO 8601 UTC end timestamp
  • ip: required device IP selector

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text:
• response uses a top-level data array
  • key item fields include dest_ip, dest_port, dest_urls, avg_app_latency, concat_app_ids, concat_app_displaynames, concat_policies, and concat_peers

docs.title: v1.monitoring.get_devices_totals
docs.text: Returns device totals for a gateway and time range.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• gateway_id (gatewayId): required gateway ID
  • child_tenant_id (childTenantId): optional child tenant selector
  • start_datetime (startDatetime): required ISO 8601 UTC start timestamp
  • end_datetime (endDatetime): required ISO 8601 UTC end timestamp

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text:
• response uses a top-level data array
  • key item fields include name, mac_addr, ipv4, vnat_ips, flows_number, rx_bytes, tx_bytes, rx_packets, and tx_packets

docs.title: v1.monitoring.get_interfaces_latest
docs.text: Returns the latest interface overview for a gateway.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• gateway_id (gatewayId): required gateway ID
  • child_tenant_id (childTenantId): optional child tenant selector

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text:
• response uses a top-level data array
  • key item fields include public_ip, cidr_ip, isp, type, state, bytes_rx, bytes_tx, packets_rx, packets_tx, cellular_stats, and wifi_stats

docs.title: v1.monitoring.get_paths_latest
docs.text: Returns the latest path overview for a gateway.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• gateway_id (gatewayId): required gateway ID
  • child_tenant_id (childTenantId): optional child tenant selector

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text:
• response uses a top-level data array
  • key item fields include link, local_ip, overlay_ip, overlay_ip_name, latency, jitter, loss_rx, loss_tx, bytes_rx, bytes_tx, packets_rx, and packets_tx

docs.title: v1.monitoring.get_routes_latest
docs.text: Returns the latest route overview for a gateway.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• gateway_id (gatewayId): required gateway ID
  • child_tenant_id (childTenantId): optional child tenant selector

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text:
• response uses a top-level data array
  • key item fields include gateway_ip, gateway_name, interface_name, metric, netmask, and route_type

docs.title: v1.monitoring.get_system_load
docs.text: Returns system load data for a gateway and time range.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• gateway_id (gatewayId): required gateway ID
  • child_tenant_id (childTenantId): optional child tenant selector
  • start_datetime (startDatetime): required ISO 8601 UTC start timestamp
  • end_datetime (endDatetime): required ISO 8601 UTC end timestamp
  • time_slots (timeSlots): optional number of buckets to return

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text:
• response uses a top-level data field
  • the payload is a time-series object defined by the v1 monitoring schema

docs.title: v1.monitoring.get_system_lte
docs.text: Returns LTE system data for a gateway and time range.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• gateway_id (gatewayId): required gateway ID
  • child_tenant_id (childTenantId): optional child tenant selector
  • start_datetime (startDatetime): required ISO 8601 UTC start timestamp
  • end_datetime (endDatetime): required ISO 8601 UTC end timestamp
  • time_slots (timeSlots): optional number of buckets to return

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text:
• response uses a top-level data field
  • the payload is a time-series object defined by the v1 monitoring schema

docs.title: v1.monitoring.get_system_memory
docs.text: Returns system memory data for a gateway and time range.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• gateway_id (gatewayId): required gateway ID
  • child_tenant_id (childTenantId): optional child tenant selector
  • start_datetime (startDatetime): required ISO 8601 UTC start timestamp
  • end_datetime (endDatetime): required ISO 8601 UTC end timestamp
  • time_slots (timeSlots): optional number of buckets to return

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text:
• response uses a top-level data field
  • the payload is a time-series object defined by the v1 monitoring schema

docs.title: v1.monitoring.get_system_uptime
docs.text: Returns system uptime data for a gateway and time range.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• gateway_id (gatewayId): required gateway ID
  • child_tenant_id (childTenantId): optional child tenant selector
  • start_datetime (startDatetime): required ISO 8601 UTC start timestamp
  • end_datetime (endDatetime): required ISO 8601 UTC end timestamp
  • time_slots (timeSlots): optional number of buckets to return

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text:
• response uses a top-level data field
  • the payload is a time-series object defined by the v1 monitoring schema

docs.title: v1.monitoring.get_system_wifi
docs.text: Returns Wi-Fi system data for a gateway and time range.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• gateway_id (gatewayId): required gateway ID
  • child_tenant_id (childTenantId): optional child tenant selector
  • start_datetime (startDatetime): required ISO 8601 UTC start timestamp
  • end_datetime (endDatetime): required ISO 8601 UTC end timestamp
  • time_slots (timeSlots): optional number of buckets to return

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text:
• response uses a top-level data field
  • the payload is a time-series object defined by the v1 monitoring schema

docs.title: v1.monitoring.get_paths_links
docs.text: Returns WAN path-link time-series data for a gateway, metric, and time range.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• gateway_id (gatewayId): required gateway ID
  • child_tenant_id (childTenantId): optional child tenant selector
  • start_datetime (startDatetime): required ISO 8601 UTC start timestamp
  • end_datetime (endDatetime): required ISO 8601 UTC end timestamp
  • metric: required WAN metric. Valid values are listed in reference-values.md
  • time_slots (timeSlots): optional number of buckets to return

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text:
• response uses a top-level data field
  • the payload is a time-series object keyed by the selected metric

docs.title: v1.monitoring.get_paths_links_totals
docs.text: Returns WAN path-link summary data for a gateway and time range.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• gateway_id (gatewayId): required gateway ID
  • child_tenant_id (childTenantId): optional child tenant selector
  • start_datetime (startDatetime): required ISO 8601 UTC start timestamp
  • end_datetime (endDatetime): required ISO 8601 UTC end timestamp

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text:
• response uses a top-level data array
  • key item fields include link, ip, isp, avg_latency, avg_jitter, avg_loss, avg_rx_throughput, avg_tx_throughput, max_latency, and max_jitter

docs.title: v1.users.get_groups
docs.text: Returns groups for a user.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• user_id (userId): required user ID
  • after_cursor (afterCursor): optional cursor for records after the current page
  • before_cursor (beforeCursor): optional cursor for records before the current page
  • max_items (maxItems): optional maximum number of results

docs.title: Response notes
docs.text:
• top-level response includes data, startCursor, endCursor, firstPage, and lastPage
  • key item fields include id, name, templateId, autoProvision, dateCreated, and dateModified
